growth_records.medium: SACC (standard amino acid complete medium), 20 amino acids, no glucose
growth_records.cite: Liu et al. Clostridium sporogenes uses reductive Stickland metabolism in the gut to generate ATP and produce circulating metabolites. Nature microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41564-022-01109-9. [PMC9089323]
growth_records.snippet: When cultured anaerobically in defined medium without carbohydrates, C. sporogenes grows rapidly, achieving a doubling time of ~45 minutes (Fig. 1a).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time of ~45 min (0.75 h) reported directly in text for growth in defined medium (SACC, 20 amino acids, 1 mM each, no glucose) under anaerobic conditions. Converted to specific growth rate: mu(h-1) = ln2 / doubling_time_h = 0.6931 / 0.75 = 0.924 h-1. Cultures maintained anaerobically at 37°C (Coy anaerobic chamber, 3.3% H2/10% CO2/85% N2, per Methods). After 24 h most amino acids are depleted and 15 metabolites accumulate (qualitative; no biomass-normalized (gDW) concentrations given, so specific uptake/secretion rates could not be calculated).
